title: Backend: Enrich compute_score.py with per-category detailed breakdown
description:
Replace the current flat justification strings in compute_score.py with a structured CategoryBreakdown object for each of the four scoring categories. Also update app/schemas.py to define the new model.

Do NOT touch generate_explanation.py or prompts.py — those are handled in task-9.

--- SCHEMA CHANGES (app/schemas.py) ---

Add a new Pydantic model BEFORE ScoreBreakdown:

```python
class CategoryBreakdown(BaseModel):
    reasoning: str           # HOW the score was calculated (formula + inputs used)
    gap_analysis: str        # What is missing to reach maximum points for this category
    improvements: list[str]  # 1-3 specific actionable items (plain strings, no bullet prefix)
    short_learning_path: list[str]  # 1-2 quick wins achievable in weeks to 1-2 months
    long_learning_path: list[str]   # 1-2 strategic improvements over 3-6 months
```

Update ScoreBreakdown:
- Change justifications field type from dict[str, str] to dict[str, CategoryBreakdown]
- Update the Field description accordingly
- The model_validator (total matches sum) stays unchanged

--- SCORING LOGIC CHANGES (app/tasks/compute_score.py) ---

Each helper function currently returns tuple[int, str] (score + justification string). Change each to return tuple[int, CategoryBreakdown] and populate all five fields.

Detailed requirements per category:

1. _compute_experience_score(experience_years, max_pts) -> tuple[int, CategoryBreakdown]
   - reasoning: f"{experience_years:.1f} years detected across CV roles. Formula: min({int(max_pts)}, {experience_years:.1f} x {max_pts/12.0:.2f}) = {raw:.2f} -> {score}"
   - gap_analysis: if score < max_pts: f"Need {target_years:.1f} total years for maximum {int(max_pts)} pts. Current gap: {gap:.1f} years." else: "Maximum score achieved."
     (target_years = 12.0 when max_pts = 30, i.e. max_pts / (max_pts/12.0))
   - improvements: ["List all freelance, contract, and part-time roles with explicit start/end dates.", "Include internships and relevant side projects with quantified outcomes."]
   - short_learning_path: ["Audit CV for any missing date ranges on existing roles and add them — this can recover hidden years of experience immediately."]
   - long_learning_path: [f"Accumulate {gap:.1f} more years of progressive hands-on experience to reach the maximum score." if gap > 0 else "No long-term experience gap — maintain current trajectory."]

2. _compute_skills_score(skills, job_description, max_pts) -> tuple[int, CategoryBreakdown, bool]
   - reasoning: build a string that includes: number of skills detected, base formula min(max_pts, len(skills) * (max_pts/20.0)), and if job_description is provided also the JD match count and boost calculation.
     Example with JD: f"{len(skills)} skills detected. Base: min({int(max_pts)}, {len(skills)} x {max_pts/20.0:.2f}) = {round(base)}. JD match: {matched}/{len(jd_words)} words -> boost +{jd_boost:.1f}. Total: {score}/{int(max_pts)}."
     Example without JD: f"{len(skills)} skills detected. Formula: min({int(max_pts)}, {len(skills)} x {max_pts/20.0:.2f}) = {score}/{int(max_pts)}."
   - gap_analysis: if score < max_pts: f"Need {needed_skills} additional in-demand skills to approach maximum. Current: {len(skills)} skills." (needed_skills = ceil((max_pts - base) / (max_pts/20.0))) else: "Maximum base score achieved."
   - improvements: ["Add specific versions and proficiency levels to skills (e.g. 'Python 3.12 — 5 years').", "Include tools and frameworks mentioned in target job descriptions to improve JD match score.", "Remove generic skills (e.g. 'Microsoft Office') and replace with technical differentiators."]
   - short_learning_path: ["Identify the top 3 skills from the target job description that you have but haven't listed — add them immediately.", "Complete one online certification in a high-demand skill to add a credentialed entry to the skills list."]
   - long_learning_path: ["Build one end-to-end project using 2-3 currently missing in-demand skills and publish it publicly (GitHub, blog post).", "Pursue a cloud or architecture certification (e.g. AWS Solutions Architect, CKA) over a 3-6 month study plan."]

3. _compute_education_score(education_level, max_pts) -> tuple[int, CategoryBreakdown]
   - reasoning: f"{education_level.title()} degree detected. Tier mapping: phd={int(max_pts)}, master={round(max_pts*0.85)}, bachelor={round(max_pts*0.65)}, other={round(max_pts*0.40)}. Score: {score}/{int(max_pts)}."
   - gap_analysis:
     - if phd: "Maximum education score achieved."
     - if master: f"A PhD would add {int(max_pts) - score} pts. Master's is already a strong signal."
     - if bachelor: f"A master's degree would add {round(max_pts*0.85) - score} pts ({round(max_pts*0.85)}/{int(max_pts)})."
     - else: f"A bachelor's degree would add {round(max_pts*0.65) - score} pts ({round(max_pts*0.65)}/{int(max_pts)})."
   - improvements:
     - if phd: ["Education score is maxed — no action needed."]
     - if master or bachelor: ["Highlight thesis or capstone project topics directly relevant to target roles.", "List relevant online courses, bootcamps, or MOOCs under education to supplement formal degree."]
     - else: ["List any completed online courses or bootcamps under education.", "Consider enrolling in a part-time bachelor's programme if long-term career growth is a priority."]
   - short_learning_path:
     - if phd: ["No short-term education action required."]
     - else: ["Add any relevant professional development courses or certifications under the education section."]
   - long_learning_path:
     - if phd or master: ["Education ceiling reached — invest long-term effort in publications, conference talks, or open-source leadership instead."]
     - if bachelor: ["Consider a part-time or online master's programme (1.5-2 years) to reach the next tier."]
     - else: ["Pursue a bachelor's or equivalent accredited qualification if career trajectory requires formal credentials."]

4. _compute_role_seniority_score(role_titles, has_management_indicators, max_pts) -> tuple[int, CategoryBreakdown]
   - reasoning: include the detected tier (senior/lead, mid-level, junior, unclassified), management bonus presence, and the numeric calculation.
     Example: f"Titles: {role_titles}. Detected tier: {tier} -> base {round(base)}/{int(max_pts)}. Management indicators: {'yes' if has_management_indicators else 'no'} (+{round(management_bonus)} pts). Total: {score}/{int(max_pts)}."
   - gap_analysis:
     - if score == int(max_pts): "Maximum role seniority score achieved."
     - elif has_management_indicators: f"Score is {score}/{int(max_pts)}. Moving to a principal, VP, or C-level title would add further points."
     - else: f"Score is {score}/{int(max_pts)}. Adding a senior/lead title and demonstrating management experience would add up to {int(max_pts) - score} pts."
   - improvements:
     - ["Use explicit seniority keywords in job titles (Senior, Lead, Principal, Staff, Architect) where accurate.", "Quantify team leadership: 'Led a team of 5 engineers' is a strong management indicator.", "List promotions explicitly as separate roles to show career progression."]
   - short_learning_path: ["Rewrite current title line to include seniority level if the role warrants it (check with employer if needed).", "Add a one-line summary under each role that mentions team size, scope, or cross-functional influence."]
   - long_learning_path: ["Target a lead or principal-level role at the next job change — even informally leading a workstream creates legitimate title advancement.", "Volunteer to mentor junior engineers or lead a project to build a management track record over 6-12 months."]

--- COMPUTE_SCORE TASK FUNCTION ---

Update the ScoreBreakdown construction in the compute_score Celery task:
- exp_score, exp_breakdown = _compute_experience_score(...)
- skills_score, skills_breakdown, job_fit_adjusted = _compute_skills_score(...)
- edu_score, edu_breakdown = _compute_education_score(...)
- role_score, role_breakdown = _compute_role_seniority_score(...)
- Pass CategoryBreakdown instances into justifications dict (not strings)

The breakdown.model_dump() call at context['score_breakdown'] remains — Pydantic will serialize CategoryBreakdown objects correctly.

--- ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA ---
- app/schemas.py has CategoryBreakdown model with all five fields.
- ScoreBreakdown.justifications type is dict[str, CategoryBreakdown].
- All four helper functions return CategoryBreakdown with all five fields populated.
- compute_score task assembles ScoreBreakdown with CategoryBreakdown instances in justifications.
- No regression: total score calculation is unchanged.
- The model_validator on ScoreBreakdown still passes (it checks integer scores, not justifications).
- The pipeline context['score_breakdown'] key contains a fully serialized dict with nested CategoryBreakdown dicts after model_dump().
